headings_flat: **3\. Component A: Core Career Sessions (6 Sessions)**
**4\. Component B: Optional ECBA Certification Track (5–6 Weeks)**
**5\. Component C: Optional Leadership Track (6 Sessions)**
**6\. Component D: Optional Mentorship Component (8–12 Weeks)**
**SECTION 1 — Program Vision, Purpose, and Strategic Alignment (Detailed)**
**Grow engagement and membership through high-value programming**
**Build partnerships with employers, recruiters, and professional associations**
**Lead the region in AI-enabled professional development**
**SECTION 3 — Core Career Sessions (6 Sessions, Fully Detailed)**
**SECTION 3 — Core Career Sessions (6 Sessions, Fully Detailed)**
**ECBA Week 5 — Solution Evaluation + Exam Prep Review**
**ECBA Week 6 (Optional) — Full Practice Exam + Strategy Lab**

sections_html.1. Program Vision & Purpose:
The Career Accelerator is the chapter’s flagship, multi-track development program designed to:

  • Strengthen career readiness for analysts and analyst-adjacent professionals
  • Provide practical learning, certification, and leadership pathways
  • Integrate AI tools into professional development
  • Expand access and community through mentorship and scholarships
  • Connect participants with senior professionals, recruiters, and employers
  • Position the chapter as the region’s innovation leader in “Skills Not Titles” programming
sections_html.2. Program Structure: The program now consists of four program components supported by marketing, sponsorship, and events:
sections_html.Core Components:
A. Core Career Sessions (6 sessions)
B. Optional ECBA Certification Track (5–6 weeks)
C. Optional Leadership Track (6 sessions)
D. Optional Mentorship Component (8–12 weeks) ← NEW
sections_html.Support Pillars:
E. Marketing & Outreach
F. Sponsorship & Events

Components run sequentially, staggered, or independently based on volunteer capacity.
sections_html.3. Component A: Core Career Sessions (6: These sessions build foundational career development skills.
sections_html.SESSION 1 — Career Mapping (Persona + Gr:
Career Mapping Grid:

  • X-axis: Functional → Technical → Management
  • Y-axis: Industry/Vertical (Retail, Healthcare, Logistics, Finance, Nonprofit, Tech, etc.)

Participants identify:

  • Current quadrant
  • Target quadrant
  • Target industries
  • Skills roadmap
  • Personas (first job, career changer, mid-career, late career)

Outputs: Career Development Plan, AI-generated pathways, study track selection.
sections_html.SESSION 2 — Resume & LinkedIn Lab:
Purpose:
Equip participants with a job-ready resume and strong LinkedIn presence aligned to their grid position and target industry.
sections_html.SESSION 3 — Mock Interviews: Behavioral + technical interviews tailored to grid quadrants.
sections_html.SESSION 4 — Problem-Solving Challenge:
Purpose:
Allow participants to demonstrate critical thinking, teamwork, facilitation, and solution framing skills.
sections_html.SESSION 5 — Requirements Elicitation Wor: Hands-on user stories, acceptance criteria, process mapping, and elicitation practice.
sections_html.SESSION 6 — Capstone Networking Mixer:
Purpose:
Expand professional networks, strengthen community bonds, and celebrate program milestones.
sections_html.4. Component B: Optional ECBA Certificat:
Weekly exam-prep cohort covering BABOK® foundations.

Outputs:

  • ECBA Roadmap
  • BABOK-aligned scenarios
  • Weekly quizzes
  • Practice questions
  • ECBA Prep Completion Badge
sections_html.5. Component C: Optional Leadership Trac:
Micro-course for communication, facilitation, influence, and conflict navigation.

Outputs:

  • Leadership profile
  • Influence map
  • Facilitation framework
  • Leadership storytelling portfolio
  • Leadership Foundations Badge
sections_html.6. Component D: Optional Mentorship Comp: This optional layer strengthens networking, accountability, and role modeling across the program.
sections_html.Purpose of the Mentorship Track:
• Provide sustained, personalized development
  • Improve confidence and professional identity
  • Strengthen application of skills between sessions
  • Support underrepresented / scholarship recipients
  • Extend the chapter’s community impact
sections_html.Format Options:
• 1:1 Mentor Pairing (recommended for scholarship recipients)
  • 2:1 or 3:1 Pods (for broader scalability)
sections_html.Length: • 8–12 weeks (runs in parallel with or directly after the Core Sessions)
sections_html.Cadence:
• Bi-weekly 30–45 minute check-ins
  • Optional monthly “Mentor Huddles” with all participants
sections_html.Mentor Profiles:
• Senior BAs
  • PMs / Product Managers
  • Data/Technical Leads
  • HR / Talent Partners
  • Industry-specific mentors (Retail, Healthcare, Logistics, Finance)

Mentors drawn from partner organizations and sponsor networks.
sections_html.For Mentees:
• Personalized Career Action Plan (refined across meetings)
  • Updated resume or portfolio
  • 1–2 completed mock interviews
  • Refined leadership stories or requirements samples
  • Industry/role-specific insights
  • Expanded professional network
  • Optional: ECBA readiness or leadership growth summary
sections_html.For Mentors:
• Impact documentation (for employer/sponsor visibility)
  • Clear expectations and topic guides
  • Opportunities to support the chapter and strengthen the profession
sections_html.Mentorship Events:
• Mentor Orientation (before start)
  • Mid-Program Mentor Meetup
  • Mentor–Mentee Mixer (aligned with Capstone Networking event)
  • Recognition at AGM
sections_html.Mentorship KPIs:
• Mentor participation (≥10 mentors)
  • Mentee satisfaction (≥4/5)
  • of meetings attended
  • Demonstrated skill growth (pre vs post self-assessment)
  • of mentor-driven referrals or introductions
  • of mentees who complete optional tracks (Leadership, ECBA)
sections_html.Goals:
• Recruit Full Track and Drop-In attendees
  • Highlight mentorship & scholarship pathways
  • Promote sponsor involvement
  • Increase chapter visibility across LinkedIn, newsletters, and partner networks
sections_html.Deliverables:
• Badge visibility campaign
  • Participant success stories (written + video)
  • Sponsor appreciation posts
  • “Career Accelerator by the Numbers” infographic
  • Newsletter recap
  • Promote the Pilot Report
  • AGM highlight reel
  • Launch 2027 interest form
sections_html.Marketing Metrics:
• LinkedIn CTR ≥10%
  • Newsletter open rate uplift
  • Badge visibility within 30 days
  • Sponsorship mentions and engagement
sections_html.Sponsorship Roles:
• Anchor Sponsor:
    • Financial support
    • Senior facilitator participation
    • Host in-person mixer
    • Brand placement
  • Professional Partners:
    • Recruiters
    • Hiring managers
    • HR business partners
  • Scholarship Sponsors:
    • Fund 5–10 scholarships
    • Provide targeted support for underrepresented participants
sections_html.Event Portfolio:
• Virtual Kickoff
  • Mid-Program Networking Hour
  • Capstone Mixer (virtual or in-person)
  • Mentor–Mentee Mixer
  • Facilitator Appreciation
  • AGM Recap Spotlight
sections_html.Full Track:
Includes:

  • All 6 Core Sessions
    • At least one optional track (ECBA or Leadership)
    • Mentorship (optional add-on)
  • Earns: Career Accelerator Badge
sections_html.Drop-In:
Attend any individual Core Session; no main badge.
Badges earned only for ECBA or Leadership Tracks.
sections_html.Mentorship Add-On:
Available to:

  • Full Track participants
  • Scholarship recipients
  • ECBA + Leadership participants (if capacity allows)
sections_html.Q1 — Prep:
• Finalize curriculum
  • Mentor recruitment
  • Sponsor recruitment
  • Marketing campaign
  • Registration + scholarships open
  • Build AI prompt libraries
  • Prepare facilitator scripts
sections_html.Q2–Q3 — Delivery:
• Run Core Sessions
  • ECBA Track
  • Leadership Track
  • Mentorship Component
  • Mid-Program Pulse Survey
  • Networking events
sections_html.Q3–Q4 — Evaluation:
• End-of-program survey
  • Mentor/Mentee feedback
  • KPI analysis
  • Pilot Report
  • Sponsor recognition
  • AGM presentation
  • 2027 roadmap creation
sections_html.Primary KPIs:
• Marketing CTR ≥ 10%
  • Registration numbers (Full Track + Drop-In)
  • Scholarship applications (5–10 recipients)
  • Post visibility and reactions
sections_html.Secondary KPIs:
• Badge visibility on LinkedIn within 30 days
  • Newsletter open rate uplift
  • Increase in chapter LinkedIn followers
  • Sponsor post engagement
  • Cross-association engagement (PMI, SHRM, etc.)
sections_html.ChatGPT said::
Below is SECTION 9 — Program Phases & Timeline (Fully Detailed).
When you're ready, say “Next Section” for Section 10 (Unified Success Metrics).
sections_html.1.1 Program Vision:
The Career Accelerator is designed to be the region’s premier career mobility program for professionals in business analysis and adjacent roles. It aligns with IIBA’s “Skills Not Titles” philosophy by focusing on practical skills, employability, and community building rather than job titles or seniority.

The vision is to create a sustainable, repeatable, high-impact development pipeline that:

  • Connects early-career and transitioning professionals with real opportunities.
  • Provides clear pathways into BA, PM, Product, Data, and Leadership roles.
  • Integrates AI skills as a core element of modern professional competence.
  • Builds a vibrant, inclusive professional community across East Tennessee.
sections_html.1.2 Program Purpose: The Career Accelerator exists to solve three problems observed across members, employers, and the regional talent market:
sections_html.1. Skill Gaps & Limited Practical Experi:
Many aspiring analysts and career changers struggle to demonstrate the practical competencies employers look for—requirements analysis, facilitation, communication, and problem-solving.

The program provides hands-on sessions, scenario-based workshops, mentorship, and resume/interview support to close these gaps.
sections_html.2. Lack of Clear Professional Pathways:
Participants often do not know:

  • What roles exist
  • How to move between functional, technical, and managerial tracks
  • How to transition across industries (verticals)

The Career Mapping Grid (Functional → Technical → Management × Industry) gives participants a clear, structured lens for career planning.
sections_html.3. Limited Networking and Employer Acces:
Without a professional network, job mobility suffers.
The Career Accelerator brings in:

  • Recruiters
  • Senior BAs, PMs, POs
  • Employers and sponsors

…and gives participants direct, repeated access to decision-makers.
sections_html.1.3 Strategic Alignment: The Career Accelerator directly supports key chapter priorities:
sections_html.Grow engagement and membership through h:
• Structured program with multi-track options (Core, ECBA, Leadership, Mentorship)
  • Targeted networking opportunities
  • High-visibility events and mixers
sections_html.Improve career readiness and certificati:
• Dedicated ECBA prep track
  • Practical application across all sessions
  • Resume, LinkedIn, mock interviews, and portfolio deliverables
sections_html.Build partnerships with employers, recru:
• Facilitator participation from senior professionals
  • Recruiter-led resume and interview labs
  • PMI / employer / university cross-promotion
sections_html.Increase accessibility and inclusion:
• Scholarships (5–10)
  • Optional mentorship expansion
  • Persona-based marketing (first job, career changer, mid-career, late-career)
sections_html.Lead the region in AI-enabled profession:
• AI tools integrated into every session
  • AI prompt libraries for career, ECBA, and leadership exercises
  • Measurable AI adoption KPI (≥70%)
sections_html.1.4 Program Principles: The Career Accelerator operates on six core principles:
sections_html.1. Practical First: Every session produces a real deliverable (resume, requirements document, interview feedback).
sections_html.4. Community-Centered: Built around senior professional engagement, partnerships, and mixers.
sections_html.5. AI-Integrated: Participants use AI tools for resumes, interview prep, requirements, career mapping, and learning tasks.
sections_html.6. Measurable Impact:
Success is defined by KPIs:

  • Completion
  • Engagement
  • Networking
  • AI adoption
  • Badge visibility
  • Scholarship impact
sections_html.1.5 Outcomes & Impact Targets: By the end of 2026, the program aims to produce:
sections_html.Participant-Level Outcomes:
• Clarified career direction
  • Updated resume and LinkedIn profile
  • Improved interviewing skills
  • Basic requirements and documentation competency
  • Expanded professional network
  • ECBA/Leadership track readiness
  • Long-term mentor relationships
sections_html.Chapter-Level Outcomes:
• Increased membership engagement
  • Stronger employer partnerships
  • Increased visibility and reputation
  • Documented pilot outcomes to scale in 2027
sections_html.1.6 What’s Unique About This Program:
• Multi-track flexibility: Full Track, ECBA, Leadership, Mentorship, Drop-In
  • Career Mapping Grid: Functional–Technical–Management × Industry
  • AI-powered learning: Prompts embedded in every module
  • Mentorship Option: Structured 8–12 week add-on
  • Sponsor-Integrated: Employers engage directly with participants
  • Practical deliverables: Not theory—skill-building with outputs

If this captures the intended direction, say “Next Section” and I will generate:
sections_html.SECTION 2 — Program Structure & Particip: The Career Accelerator is designed as a flexible, modular, multi-track program that can support participants with different goals, levels of experience, schedules, and learning preferences. Tracks may run sequentially or independently, depending on volunteer capacity and sponsorship commitments.
sections_html.2.1 Program Architecture Overview: The Career Accelerator includes four core components:
sections_html.A. Core Career Sessions (6 sessions): A foundational set of practical career development workshops delivered virtually.
sections_html.B. Optional ECBA Certification Track (5–: Exam-aligned learning for participants pursuing a recognized BA certification.
sections_html.C. Optional Leadership Track (6 sessions: A micro-course for early-career professionals developing communication, influence, and facilitation skills.
sections_html.D. Optional Mentorship Component (8–12 w: Structured 1:1 or small-pod mentorship layered on top of any track.
sections_html.Flexible Not Concurrent Model:
• Tracks do not need to run at the same time.
  • Core Sessions serve as the anchor for each cohort.
  • ECBA and Leadership Tracks may run before, after, or between core sessions.
  • Mentorship can run parallel to any track or extend after it.

This structure reduces volunteer load and improves long-term sustainability.
sections_html.2.3 Participation Models: Participants may choose their level of involvement:
sections_html.Model 1 — Full Track (Flagship Experienc:
Designed for participants seeking a complete, structured development arc.

Includes:

  • All 6 Core Sessions
    • One Optional Track (ECBA OR Leadership)
    • Capstone Networking Mixer
    • Access to Mentorship Track (optional)

Deliverables:

  • Career Development Plan
  • Updated Resume & LinkedIn
  • Mock Interview Feedback
  • Requirements Documentation Sample
  • Networking Contacts
  • Optional: ECBA or Leadership Badge

Credential Earned:
sections_html.Career Accelerator Completion Badge:
Ideal for:

  • Early career professionals
  • Career changers
  • Professionals targeting promotions or role transitions
  • Scholarship recipients
sections_html.Model 2 — Drop-In Participation:
Participants attend any of the six core sessions without commitment.

Includes:

  • Access to any single Core Session
  • Access to associated templates and worksheets
  • Access to Career Mapping tools

Deliverables:

  • Session-specific exercises only

Credential Earned:

  • None (unless the participant completes an optional track)

Ideal for:

  • Members with limited time
  • Experienced professionals seeking refreshers
  • Those exploring whether to pursue the Full Track next year
sections_html.Model 3 — Certification Track Participan:
Participants enroll in the ECBA Track only.

Includes:

  • All ECBA sessions
  • Study roadmap
  • Weekly quizzes
  • Practice exam
  • Optional mentorship pairing
  • Access to Career Mapping outputs when attending Session 1 (recommended)

Credential Earned:
sections_html.ECBA Prep Completion Badge:
Ideal for:

  • Entry-level analysts
  • Career changers
  • Students
  • Professionals wanting structured exam prep
sections_html.Model 4 — Leadership Track Participant (:
Participants enroll solely in the Leadership Track.

Includes:

  • All 6 leadership sessions
  • Leader profile assessment
  • Influence maps
  • Facilitated communication practice
  • Optional mentorship pairing

Credential Earned:
sections_html.Leadership Foundations Badge:
Ideal for:

  • Aspiring managers
  • Analysts transitioning to lead roles
  • PM/PO professionals building influence and clarity
  • Mid-career professionals seeking advancement
sections_html.Model 5 — Mentorship Add-On Participant:
Participants opt into the 8–12 week Mentorship Component.

Format Options:

  • 1:1 pairing
  • 2–3 person mentorship pods

Includes:

  • Personalized guidance
  • Accountability to goals
  • Review of resumes, stories, examples
  • Role-specific advice (based on career mapping quadrant)
  • Industry-specific insights (based on vertical selection)

Credential Earned:

  • None (mentorship is an enhancement, not a credential)

Ideal for:

  • Full Track participants
  • Scholarship recipients
  • ECBA or Leadership participants seeking extra support
sections_html.Step 1 — Registration: Choose Full Track, optional tracks, or Drop-In.
sections_html.Step 2 — Career Mapping: All Full Track participants start with Session 1 (Career Mapping Grid).
sections_html.Step 5 — Mentorship (Optional): Pairing begins mid-program and continues into the post-program window.
sections_html.Step 7 — Badges + LinkedIn Visibility: Full Track, ECBA, and Leadership badges awarded.
sections_html.Step 8 — Post-Program Impact: Participants build career momentum, schedule ECBA exams, pursue roles, or advance internally.
sections_html.Target Enrollment for Pilot Year:
• Full Track: ≥ 10 participants
  • Drop-In: 20–40 participants across sessions
  • ECBA Track: 10–20 participants
  • Leadership Track: 10–15 participants
  • Mentorship Component: 10–15 mentees, 10+ mentors

Over time, these targets can scale as sponsorship and volunteer capacity grow.
sections_html.2.6 Accessibility & Inclusion Focus:
The program is deliberately designed to minimize barriers:

  • Scholarships for 5–10 participants
  • Flexible attendance
  • Virtual-first delivery
  • Accessible formats (slides, templates, transcripts)
  • Clear pathways for career changers
  • AI tools to assist participants with neurodivergence, ESL, or limited experience
sections_html.2.7 How This Structure Supports KPIs:
• Participant Completion ≥ 80%:
    Modular structure with reminders and mentorship improves retention.
  • Engagement Score ≥ 4/5:
    Applied learning and hands-on deliverables improve perceived value.
  • Enrollment ≥ 10 Full Track:
    Multiple pathways + scholarships widen appeal.
  • Facilitator Participation:
    Tracks bring in senior professionals, recruiters, and sponsors naturally.
  • Networking Outcomes:
    Mentorship + mixers + group work create repeat connection points.
  • AI Adoption ≥ 70%:
    AI prompts embedded across every track.
  • Badge Visibility:
    Clear track badges encourage LinkedIn posting.

If this section looks correct, say “Next Section” and I will generate:
sections_html.SECTION 3 — Core Career Sessions (6 Sess:
The Core Career Sessions are the anchor of the Career Accelerator.
They provide the hands-on, practical career development foundation that supports every optional track, mentorship component, and networking event.

All sessions are designed for virtual delivery, include AI integration, and result in tangible take-home deliverables.
sections_html.SESSION 1 — Career Mapping (Persona-Base:
Purpose:
Give participants a structured, personalized understanding of where they are now, where they want to go, and which roles/industries best align with their interests and skills.
sections_html.1. Career Mapping Grid Introduction:
Participants learn the 2-axis system:

X-Axis: Functional → Technical → Management

  • Functional examples: BA, PO, Ops Analyst, UX Research (lite)
  • Technical examples: Systems Analyst, Data Analyst, QA, Technical BA
  • Management examples: PM, Product Manager, Team Lead, Scrum Master

Y-Axis: Industry / Vertical

  • Retail, Healthcare, Insurance, Logistics, Finance, Education, Manufacturing
  • Public Sector, Nonprofit, Tech/SaaS, Hospitality, Energy, etc.

Participants place themselves on the grid today and identify where they want to be in 1–2 years.
sections_html.2. Persona Selection:
Participants choose the persona that fits their journey:

  • First Job / Student
  • Career Changer
  • Mid-Career Upskiller
  • Late-Career Pivot

Each persona has unique AI prompt paths and skill recommendations.
sections_html.3. Role Exploration Exercise:
Participants identify:

  • 2–3 target roles on their chosen X-axis
  • 2–3 industries on the Y-axis
  • One short-term and one long-term role goal
sections_html.4. AI-Assisted Career Roadmap:
Participants use AI to generate:

  • A personalized 90-day skill plan
  • Resume alignment suggestions
  • Recommended certifications / skills based on quadrant and industry
  • Sample job titles aligned with their grid placement
sections_html.Guest or Facilitator Role:
A senior BA/PM/PO leader shares:

  • Role evolution in their career
  • How grid-based thinking applied to their transitions
sections_html.1. Role-Aligned Resume Structure:
Functional, technical, and managerial resumes differ:

  • Functional: requirements, stakeholders, facilitation
  • Technical: systems, data, tools, integrations
  • Management: planning, prioritization, conflict handling

Participants tailor their resume accordingly.
sections_html.2. Targeted Resume Editing:
Breakouts with recruiters and senior professionals to refine:

  • Summary
  • Bullet points
  • Skills section
  • Achievements aligned with target quadrant
sections_html.3. LinkedIn Optimization:
Participants rewrite:

  • Headline
  • About section
  • First 3 experience bullets
  • “Open to Work” role settings aligned to their grid quadrant
sections_html.4. AI Assistance:
AI rewrites participants’ pitches:

  • Into professional tone
  • Into conversational tone
  • Tailored to a specific employer or industry
sections_html.SESSION 3 — Mock Interviews (Behavioral:
Purpose:
Develop confidence and fluency in answering interview questions tailored to their grid quadrant.
sections_html.2. Technical / Role-Specific Interviews:
Questions vary by quadrant:

  • Functional: requirements clarity, BA task questions
  • Technical: SQL basics, APIs, data analysis, test strategy
  • Management: conflict, prioritization, tradeoffs
sections_html.3. Live Interview Rotations:
Breakout groups rotate:

  • Interviewer
  • Interviewee
  • Observer

Each rotation includes a short debrief.
sections_html.4. AI Question Bank:
AI generates:

  • 10 behavioral questions
  • 10 technical/depth questions
  • 5 industry-aligned questions
sections_html.1. Case Study Challenge:
Examples:

  • “Membership Portal Redesign”
  • “Customer Service Process Improvement”
  • “Inventory Outage Analysis”
  • “Healthcare Intake Bottleneck”
sections_html.2. Team Problem-Solving Framework:
Teams work through:

  • Problem definition
  • Root cause analysis
  • Options analysis
  • Recommendation
sections_html.3. Presentation Development:
Teams prepare a quick:

  • 3–5 slide deck
  • Solution summary
  • Risk / impact analysis
sections_html.4. AI Support:
AI used to:

  • Generate alternative ideas
  • Identify risks and dependencies
  • Suggest metrics to evaluate solutions
sections_html.SESSION 5 — Requirements Elicitation & D:
Purpose:
Teach participants core analysis skills needed across BA, PM, PO, and operations roles.
sections_html.1. Elicitation Simulation:
Participants practice a mock stakeholder interview:

  • Asking clarifying questions
  • Identifying assumptions
  • Extracting requirements
sections_html.2. Requirements Documentation:
Participants create:

  • User stories
  • Acceptance criteria
  • Process maps (optional)
  • Lo-fi use case descriptions
sections_html.1. Pitch Practice:
Participants craft:

  • 30-second elevator pitch
  • Grid-aligned career summary
  • Industry-specific value statement
sections_html.2. Networking Circles:
Sponsor-led or facilitated breakout rotations based on:

  • Quadrant (Functional/Technical/Management)
  • Industry interest
  • Job-seeking status
sections_html.3. Mentor–Mentee Introductions: Participants meet mentors as part of the optional track.
sections_html.Hosting:
• Anchor Sponsor HQ (preferred)
  • Virtual platform if required

If this matches your expectations, say “Next Section” and I’ll generate:
sections_html.SECTION 4 — Optional ECBA Certification:
The ECBA Certification Track is an optional, 5–6 week cohort-based learning experience designed for early-career analysts, career changers, and students seeking the Entry Certificate in Business Analysis (ECBA).
This track emphasizes applied learning, scenario-based exercises, and AI-powered study tools.

The track is aligned to BABOK® v3 and the Business Analysis Standard.
sections_html.4.1 Track Objectives:
Participants will:

  • Understand core BA concepts and tasks at the ECBA level
  • Learn structured approaches to elicitation, requirements, and analysis
  • Practice applying BABOK concepts to real scenarios
  • Build exam-taking skills through comprehension checks and a final practice test
  • Create a personal study and exam scheduling plan
  • Increase confidence in pursuing analyst roles or BA-adjacent jobs
sections_html.4.2 Structure & Cadence:
• 5 core sessions + optional Week 6 practice exam
  • Weekly 60–90-minute virtual sessions
  • Weekly pre-work (light reading + quizzes)
  • AI tool integration in every session
  • Optional mentorship pairing for extra support

This track can run:

  • After the Core Career Sessions
  • Between Core Sessions
  • As a stand-alone sprint
sections_html.ECBA Week 1 — BA Foundations & BACCM: Goal: Establish the conceptual foundation for the exam and real-world practice.
sections_html.Pre-Work::
• BABOK® Ch. 7
  • 30-question practice test
  • AI prompt: “Explain solution limitations vs enterprise limitations.”
sections_html.In-Session Activities::
1. Leadership Story Frameworks
    • STAR / CAR
    • Challenge–Action–Outcome storytelling
    • Vision messaging for future-focused stories
  2. Story Refinement Workshop
    • Participants share real examples
    • Peers and facilitators provide refinement feedback
  3. AI Exercise:
    • AI rewrites:
      • Formal version
      • Casual version
      • Executive summary version
sections_html.Deliverables::
• 2–3 refined leadership stories
  • Improved leadership pitch
  • AI-enhanced story drafts
sections_html.ECBA Week 2 — Business Analysis Planning: Goal: Understand how BA work is planned and how elicitation is structured.
sections_html.ECBA Week 3 — Requirements Life Cycle +: Goal: Learn how requirements evolve and understand strategy at a recognition level.
sections_html.ECBA Week 4 — Requirements Analysis & De: Goal: Develop the skills to analyze requirements, create basic models, and ensure clarity.
sections_html.ECBA Week 5 — Solution Evaluation + Exam: Goal: Cover final BABOK content and prepare for the exam.
sections_html.ECBA Week 6 (Optional) — Full Practice E: Goal: Build exam confidence and refine remaining knowledge gaps.
sections_html.4.4 AI Integration Across the ECBA Track:
Every week includes targeted AI use, including:

  • Flashcards
  • Requirement rewrites
  • Practice questions
  • Stakeholder mapping
  • Study plans
  • Current/Future state diagrams
  • Acceptance criteria generation

This drives the ≥70% AI adoption KPI for the overall program.
sections_html.4.5 Mentorship Alignment:
Participants who opt into mentorship may receive:

  • Weekly or biweekly study accountability
  • Review of requirements, story maps, or practice questions
  • Mock interviews focused on BA mindset
  • Help refining their exam timeline
  • Industry-specific BA guidance

Mentorship increases completion and confidence rates.
sections_html.Primary Indicators:
• Completion ≥ 80%
  • Average session satisfaction ≥ 4/5
  • ECBA readiness score improvement
  • % scheduling exam within 3 months
sections_html.Secondary Indicators:
• AI adoption ≥ 70%
  • Mentor engagement
  • Scholarship recipient progress
  • Role-placement or job-transition stories

If this matches your intent, say “Next Section” and I’ll generate:
sections_html.SECTION 5 — Optional Leadership Track (F:
The Leadership Track is a 6-session micro-course that develops foundational leadership, communication, and influence skills for early-career analysts, career changers, and aspiring managers.
It pairs especially well with the Core Sessions and offers a bridge between entry-level skill-building and long-term career progression.

The track is scenario-based, practice-heavy, and AI-integrated, with exercises grounded in real BA, PM, Product, and Data contexts.
sections_html.5.1 Track Objectives:
Participants will learn to:

  • Communicate clearly and professionally in meetings and documentation
  • Facilitate conversations and create psychological safety
  • Ask high-quality questions that uncover context and assumptions
  • Navigate conflict and collaborate across teams
  • Influence stakeholders without authority
  • Make structured decisions and communicate recommendations
  • Tell leadership stories that demonstrate impact
sections_html.5.2 Structure & Cadence:
• 6 sessions, each 60–90 minutes, virtual
  • Delivered bi-weekly or monthly
  • Follows or runs parallel to Core Sessions
  • AI exercises in each session
  • Optional mentorship support for deeper practice
sections_html.Leadership Session 1 — Leader Identity &:
Purpose:
Start with self-awareness: how you show up, how you’re perceived, and what leadership means in analyst roles.
sections_html.Leadership Session 2 — Communication Cla:
Purpose:
Build practical facilitation skills and communication clarity for business settings.
sections_html.Leadership Session 3 — Leading Through Q:
Purpose:
Teach how to ask questions that improve understanding and reveal hidden assumptions.
sections_html.Leadership Session 4 — Stakeholder Influ:
Purpose:
Develop influence skills and improve collaboration with difficult personalities.
sections_html.Leadership Session 5 — Decision-Making,:
Purpose:
Help analysts make structured recommendations and weigh trade-offs.
sections_html.Leadership Session 6 — Leadership Storyt:
Purpose:
Teach participants how to articulate their impact—essential for interviews, promotions, and leadership roles.
sections_html.5.4 AI Integration Throughout the Track:
AI is used to:

  • Rewrite messages for clarity
  • Generate questions
  • Role-play difficult stakeholders
  • Build recommendation frameworks
  • Improve storytelling
  • Write leadership goal plans
  • Analyze strengths or communication patterns

Every session contains a short, structured AI prompt to ensure ≥70% AI adoption.
sections_html.5.5 Mentorship Alignment:
Participants who choose mentorship receive:

  • Leadership scenario guidance
  • Practice facilitation or conflict scenarios
  • Review of stories and communication drafts
  • Career navigation advice based on grid quadrant

Mentorship accelerates applied skill growth.
sections_html.SECTION 6 — Optional Mentorship Componen:
The Mentorship Component is an 8–12 week optional overlay that enhances participant experience, accelerates skill development, and strengthens community involvement.
It is structured, supported, flexible, and aligned with Full Track, ECBA, and Leadership participants.

Mentorship is a key differentiator that increases program credibility, retention, and networking outcomes—directly supporting multiple KPIs.
sections_html.6.1 Mentorship Purpose:
The mentorship offering exists to provide:

  • Personalized guidance and accountability
  • Role modeling and leadership exposure
  • Support with resumes, interviews, requirements, and career decisions
  • Industry-specific insights aligned with the Career Mapping Grid
  • An inclusive bridge for scholarship recipients and underrepresented groups
  • A long-term community-building structure within the chapter

It reinforces the “Skills Not Titles” and career mobility mission.
sections_html.1. One-to-One Mentorship (Recommended fo:
• Best for those with specific goals (ECBA, role transition, portfolio building)
  • Best match for high-support needs (students, career changers)
sections_html.2. Micro-Pods (2–3 Mentees per Mentor):
• Encourages peer support
  • Scales more efficiently
  • Ideal for Leadership Track participants or groups with similar goals
sections_html.3. Hybrid Model (Pod + Optional 1:1):
• Pods meet bi-weekly
  • 1:1 as needed for mock interviews or personal challenges

Flexibility allows you to adjust based on mentor availability.
sections_html.6.3 Mentor Profiles:
Mentors are drawn from:

  • Senior Business Analysts
  • Project Managers
  • Product Owners / Product Managers
  • Data Analysts / Systems Analysts
  • HR/Talent professionals
  • Sponsor organizations
  • Local PMI and IIBA senior members

Ideal mentor profile:

  • 5–20+ years of experience
  • Comfortable guiding early-career professionals
  • Available 30–45 minutes bi-weekly
sections_html.Week 0 — Matching:
• Based on: career goals, quadrant selection (Functional/Technical/Management), industry interest
  • Secondary match factors: persona type, scheduling needs, optional ECBA/Leadership track
sections_html.Week 1 — Orientation:
• Mentor training (30 minutes)
  • Mentee orientation (30 minutes)
  • Overview of: expectations, boundaries, suggested topics, templates, and the Career Mapping Grid
sections_html.Weeks 2–10 — Bi-Weekly Meetings (4–5 Ses:
Meeting themes include:

  • Career Mapping follow-up
  • Resume support and job search strategy
  • Mock interview practice
  • Requirements or BA skill development
  • Feedback on session deliverables (from Core, ECBA, Leadership tracks)
  • Role transitions and promotion planning
  • Industry-specific knowledge exchange
sections_html.Week 5 / Midpoint Check-In:
• Quick satisfaction/match survey
  • Adjust pairings if needed
  • Identify additional support
sections_html.Week 10–12 — Wrap-Up:
• Final reflection
  • Career plan refinement
  • Discuss next steps (certifications, job searching, leadership opportunities)
  • Request LinkedIn recommendations (optional)
sections_html.Mentor Guide Includes::
• Conversation starters
  • Common early-career pitfalls
  • Coaching questions
  • Tips for supporting neurodivergent or career-changing mentees
  • Boundaries & professionalism guidelines
  • Template for providing constructive feedback
sections_html.Mentee Guide Includes::
• How to prepare for meetings
  • How to set goals and track progress
  • What to ask mentors
  • Guidance on professional etiquette
  • Template for Career Action Plan
  • Accountability habits
sections_html.6.7 AI Integration in Mentorship:
Mentors may use AI to help mentees with:

  • Drafting or refining resumes
  • Creating interview questions
  • Analyzing stakeholder situations
  • Rewriting requirements, user stories, acceptance criteria
  • Summarizing career goals into actionable steps
  • Creating personalized study plans
  • Preparing industry/company research

Mentees use AI to:

  • Prepare questions for their mentor
  • Generate drafts for review
  • Reflect on strengths or blind spots

This directly supports your ≥70% AI adoption KPI.
sections_html.Core Sessions: • Mentors can review resume drafts, pitch practice scripts, and requirements documentation.
sections_html.ECBA Track: • Mentors support weekly quizzes, study schedules, BABOK interpretation, and mock exam questions.
sections_html.Leadership Track: • Mentors help with conflict navigation scenarios, leadership stories, and stakeholder influence maps.
sections_html.Sponsorship & Events: • Mentors attend mixers, roundtables, and networking events to connect with broader participant groups.
sections_html.Primary:
• Number of sponsors involved (goal: 2–4)
  • Number of senior professionals engaged (≥3)
  • Number of recruiters engaged (≥2)
  • Scholarship funding achieved (5–10 recipients)
sections_html.Secondary:
• Sponsor satisfaction score
  • Returning sponsors for next cohort
  • Sponsor LinkedIn engagement
  • Attendance at sponsor-hosted events
sections_html.Risk: Mentor–Mentee Mismatch:
Mitigation:

  • Quick midpoint survey
  • Option to re-match discreetly
  • Provide a pool of backup mentors
sections_html.Risk: Low Mentor Capacity:
Mitigation:

  • Use micro-pods
  • Recruit from sponsors
  • Tap PMI, BA managers, and local employers
  • Cap mentorship participation if necessary
sections_html.Risk: Mentee Drop-Off:
Mitigation:

  • Integrate mentorship touchpoints with Core Sessions
  • Use automated reminders
  • Provide quick wins (resume review, interview prep)
sections_html.Risk: Mentor Overload:
Mitigation:

  • Clear time expectations (4–5 meetings total)
  • Provide templates/prompts to reduce prep
  • Provide recognition and sponsor visibility

If this section looks good, say “Next Section” and I’ll generate:
sections_html.SECTION 7 — Marketing & Outreach (Fully:
Marketing & Outreach is a critical support pillar designed to drive enrollment, increase visibility, strengthen the chapter’s brand, and highlight the successes of participants, sponsors, and mentors.
It includes branding, recruitment, participant engagement, storytelling, and post-program amplification.

This component directly supports KPIs such as Enrollment, Engagement, Marketing Reach, Badge Visibility, and Facilitator Participation.
sections_html.Primary Goals:
• Secure financial support to fund 5–10 scholarships
  • Recruit senior facilitators, mentors, and guest speakers
  • Strengthen partnerships with regional employers
  • Host in-person or virtual mixers
  • Enhance the chapter’s credibility and community impact
sections_html.Secondary Goals:
• Provide employers with access to trained talent
  • Offer visibility in chapter marketing and events
  • Build a pipeline for recurring annual sponsorship
  • Promote long-term collaboration across the community
sections_html.Primary Target Audiences:
• Early-career professionals (0–5 years)
  • Career changers
  • Students and young professionals
  • Underrepresented groups (for scholarships)
  • Professionals in analyst-adjacent roles (PM, PO, Ops, QA, Data)
sections_html.Secondary Audiences:
• Employers
  • Recruiters and staffing firms
  • Higher education institutions
  • Professional associations (PMI, SHRM, etc.)
  • Sponsors and partners
sections_html.Brand Tagline:: “Accelerate Your Career — Hands-On Skills. AI-Powered Tools.”
sections_html.Brand Identity Components:
• Cohort-specific visual theme
  • Badge designs: Career Accelerator, ECBA Prep, Leadership Track
  • Social media graphics (posts, banners, reels)
  • Event banners for mixers
  • Participant quote cards
  • Sponsor recognition visuals
sections_html.Messaging Themes:
• Skills Not Titles
  • Career Transition–Friendly
  • Hands-On, Not Lecture-Based
  • AI-Integrated Professional Development
  • Community, Inclusion, Access
sections_html.Phase A — Pre-Launch (Q1 2026): Purpose: Build awareness, generate early interest, and position the program.
sections_html.Key Messages:
• Accessible (virtual, flexible, layered tracks)
  • Inclusive (scholarships, mentorship)
  • Future-ready (AI-powered learning)
  • Direct employer involvement
sections_html.Call-to-Action:
• Register for Full Track
  • Apply for scholarships
  • Attend Session 1 (Career Mapping)
  • Join upcoming info session (optional)
sections_html.Phase C — Engagement During Program (Q2–: Purpose: Maintain momentum, highlight participation, and promote community success.
sections_html.Messaging:
• Celebrate small wins
  • Share participant outputs (resumes, stories, decks—with permission)
  • Reinforce community and sponsor appreciation
sections_html.Phase D — Post-Program Amplification (Q3: Purpose: Showcase impact and encourage next cohort interest.
sections_html.Primary Channels:
• LinkedIn (highest ROI)
  • Chapter newsletter
  • MemberNova platform
  • Email campaigns
  • Partner networks (PMI Knoxville, employer newsletters)
sections_html.Content Repository:
• Google Drive shared folder
  • Canva templates
  • Linktree for mobile access
sections_html.For Core Career Sessions:
• Individual session promotional posts
  • “What You Will Learn” flyers
  • Headshots/spotlights of senior professionals
  • Session recap cards (with quotes)
sections_html.For ECBA Track:
• ECBA informational explainer
  • BABOK-aligned prompt library (marketing + content tool)
  • “Is ECBA Right for You?” post
  • Progress markers participants share (“Week 2 complete!”)
sections_html.For Leadership Track:
• Leadership teaser post (“Want to build influence?”)
  • Leadership story examples
  • Leadership badge reveal
sections_html.For Mentorship:
• Mentor recruitment posts
  • Mentor welcome kit
  • Mentee spotlight posts
  • Mentor–mentee pairing announcements (optional)
sections_html.7.7 Accessibility & Inclusion Considerat:
Marketing content will emphasize:

  • Virtual-first flexibility
  • Scholarships
  • Availability for all roles (BA, PM, PO, QA, Ops, Data)
  • Neurodivergence-friendly tools (AI frameworks, templates, reminders)
  • Clear expectations and low barriers to entry
sections_html.Risk: Low Enrollment:
• Run career-changer-heavy marketing
  • Cross-post in university and employer groups
  • Provide a single-session preview event
  • Offer early-bird incentives
sections_html.Risk: Limited Visibility:
• Increase frequency of posts
  • Use participant and facilitator amplification
  • Encourage cohort members to tag employers
sections_html.Risk: Scholarships not reaching intended:
• Targeted outreach through community groups
  • Encourage nominations by mentors
  • Provide simplified scholarship application

sections_html.SECTION 8 — Sponsorship & Events (Fully:
Sponsorship and Events form the engagement, credibility, and resourcing backbone of the Career Accelerator.
This component strengthens relationships with employers, increases chapter visibility, funds scholarships, and ensures that participants gain real-world insight from senior professionals and recruiters.

The design emphasizes mutual value: sponsors gain talent exposure, thought leadership opportunities, and brand visibility—while participants gain practical insights, networking, and career access.
sections_html.Tier 1 — Anchor Sponsor (Primary Employe:
Provides core program support.

Benefits:

  • Exclusive brand placement on all program materials
  • Recognition at kickoff, mid-program, and capstone events
  • Opportunity to host the in-person networking mixer
  • Guaranteed speaker/facilitator slots
  • Option to nominate internal mentees or mentors
  • Feature in sponsor spotlight posts
  • Employer brand visibility as community talent partner

Responsibilities:

  • Provide 1–2 senior professionals as facilitators
  • Support funding for scholarships (partial or full)
  • Provide in-person event space (optional)
sections_html.Tier 2 — Professional Development Partne:
Typically includes additional employers, staffing firms, or universities.

Benefits:

  • Guest facilitator or recruiter participation
  • Brand placement on select materials
  • Spotlight post on LinkedIn
  • Invitation to mixers
  • Priority table or breakout room at networking events

Responsibilities:

  • Provide 1 recruiter or hiring professional
  • Support mock interviews or resume reviews
sections_html.Tier 3 — Scholarship Sponsor:
Focused solely on funding participant access.

Benefits:

  • Recognition in scholarship award announcements
  • Brand on scholarship marketing materials
  • Social media acknowledgments

Responsibilities:

  • Provide funding for 1–5 scholarship recipients
sections_html.Tier 4 — Community Partner:
Non-financial but provides value through expertise, promotion, or resources.

Examples:

  • PMI Knoxville
  • Local universities
  • Community nonprofits
  • Workforce development alliances

Benefits:

  • Listed as community partner
  • Co-marketing opportunities
sections_html.Value Proposition for Employers:
• Access to early-career and transitioning talent
  • Strengthen employer brand in a professional community
  • Demonstrate commitment to workforce development
  • Low-cost way to contribute to regional talent pipelines
  • Employees gain leadership and facilitation experience as mentors/speakers
  • Builds goodwill with philanthropic positioning
sections_html.Value Proposition for Staffing Firms:
• Direct access to active job-seekers
  • Visibility during resume and interview sessions
  • Opportunity to connect talent to upcoming roles
sections_html.Value Proposition for Higher Education:
• Provide development support to students or recent graduates
  • Promote pathways into BA/PM/product careers
sections_html.8.4 Events Portfolio (All Detailed): Events are embedded throughout the program to build community and drive engagement.
sections_html.Event 1 — Program Kickoff (Virtual):
Purpose:
Welcome participants, explain tracks, highlight sponsors, and outline the Career Mapping Framework.

Components:

  • Welcome message
  • Sponsor spotlight
  • Program overview
  • Facilitation team introductions
  • Quick breakout for cohort bonding
sections_html.Event 2 — Mid-Program Networking Hour (V:
Purpose:
Boost retention, build connections, and identify early success stories.

Components:

  • Short panel: “What Employers Look for in Analysts Today”
  • Grid-based networking rooms (Functional/Technical/Management)
  • Sponsor-hosted breakout room
  • Optional: speed networking rotation
sections_html.Event 3 — Capstone Networking Mixer (Vir:
This is the signature event of the Career Accelerator.

Purpose:
Connect participants directly with employers, recruiters, and senior professionals.

Components:

  • Participant elevator pitches
  • Industry vertical breakout rooms
  • Recruiter Q&A
  • Sponsor showcase table or virtual booth
  • Mentor–mentee introduction (if participating in mentorship track)
  • Badge award announcements

Ideal Venue:

  • Anchor Sponsor’s conference space
  • Or virtual event with multiple breakout channels
sections_html.Event 4 — Mentor–Mentee Mixer (Optional,:
Purpose:
Build community among mentorship participants and align expectations.

Components:

  • Pair introductions
  • Mentorship playbook walkthrough
  • Quick rapport-building breakout activities
  • Success stories from prior mentors
sections_html.Event 5 — Facilitator Appreciation Event:
Purpose:
Recognize facilitators, recruiters, mentors, and sponsors for their contributions.

Components:

  • Certificates
  • Public recognition at AGM
  • Optional sponsor gift
sections_html.Event 6 — AGM Spotlight (Annual General:
Purpose:
Report Career Accelerator results and promote next year’s cohort.

Components:

  • Success stories
  • Scholarship highlights
  • Sponsor appreciation
  • Pilot Report presentation
  • Early sign-up interest form
sections_html.8.5 Sponsor Engagement in Sessions:
Sponsors or partner professionals may participate in:

  • Resume reviews
  • Mock interviews
  • Problem-solving panels
  • Industry-specific breakout rooms
  • Leadership storytelling
  • Facilitator guest spots
  • ECBA strategy discussions (optional)

This supports the KPI for ≥2 recruiters + ≥3 senior professionals actively engaged.
sections_html.Risk: Sponsor Attrition or Insufficient:
Mitigation:

  • Diversify sponsor pool
  • Seek multiple Scholarship Sponsors
  • Offer micro-sponsorship options
sections_html.Risk: Low Sponsor Engagement:
Mitigation:

  • Clear scheduling and expectations
  • Easy-to-prepare facilitator templates
  • Backup senior professionals available
sections_html.Risk: Scheduling Conflicts:
Mitigation:

  • Multiple date options for guest-led sessions
  • Pre-recorded sponsor messages if needed

sections_html.SECTION 9 — Program Phases & Timeline (F: The Career Accelerator operates across three major phases throughout the year—Prep, Delivery, and Evaluation—to allow for structured planning, high-quality execution, and measurable impact. These phases also support staggered scheduling of Core Sessions, Optional Tracks, Mentorship, and Events.
sections_html.9.1 Annual Program Overview Timeline:
A recommended annual pacing (adjustable as needed):

Month

Activities

January–March (Q1)

Phase 1: Prep — Curriculum, materials, mentors, sponsors, marketing, scholarships

April–August (Q2–Q3)

Phase 2: Delivery — Core Sessions + ECBA + Leadership + Mentorship + Events

September–November (Q3–Q4)

Phase 3: Evaluation — Surveys, KPIs, Pilot Report, AGM presentation

December

Planning for next cohort, sponsor renewals

This ensures an annual rhythm that chapters, sponsors, and volunteers can reliably plan around.
sections_html.9.2 Phase 1 — PREP (Q1 2026): Goal: Build strong program foundations through deliberate design, recruitment, and early visibility.
sections_html.9.2.1 Curriculum & Materials Development:
Core Career Sessions

  • Finalize facilitator scripts
  • Build activity worksheets and templates
  • Create industry-tailored examples for Career Mapping
  • Prepare AI Prompt Libraries for each session

Optional ECBA Track

  • Finalize detailed weekly outlines
  • Build weekly quizzes + scenario exercises
  • Prepare practice exam materials
  • Create ECBA study roadmap template

Optional Leadership Track

  • Create leadership exercises and frameworks
  • Build stakeholder influence map templates
  • Prepare communication clarity examples
  • Create AI prompts for leadership storytelling

Mentorship Component

  • Finalize Mentorship Guidebooks (mentor + mentee versions)
  • Create Career Action Plan templates
  • Prepare mentor training slides
sections_html.9.2.2 Sponsorship & Facilitator Recruitm:
• Confirm Anchor Sponsor and Professional Development Partners
  • Secure scholarship funding commitments
  • Recruit facilitators (≥3 senior professionals + ≥2 recruiters)
  • Recruit mentors (≥10)
  • Define sponsor participation roles per session
  • Gather headshots, bios, and logos for marketing assets
sections_html.9.2.3 Marketing Launch (Early Q1):
Deliverables:

  • Program announcement post
  • Scholarship announcement
  • Persona-based flyers (career changer, student, mid-career)
  • Facilitator & mentor spotlight posts
  • Event listings on LinkedIn and MemberNova
  • “What is the Career Accelerator?” video teaser

Registration opens:

  • Full Track
  • ECBA Track
  • Leadership Track
  • Scholarship applications

tables: Phase 1: Prep — Curriculum, materials, mentors, sponsors, marketing, scholarships
Phase 2: Delivery — Core Sessions + ECBA + Leadership + Mentorship + Events
Phase 3: Evaluation — Surveys, KPIs, Pilot Report, AGM presentation
Planning for next cohort, sponsor renewals
